modules.title: 🌙 Indicator Lab 1: Volatility & Mean Reversion (Bollinger Bands)
modules.description: Welcome to the Strategy Track. Tonight we focus on mathematically quantifying volatility to find mean-reversion setups.

fields.name: 📉 The Mathematics of the Bands
fields.value: Bollinger Bands plot two standard deviations away from a Simple Moving Average (SMA). When price pierces the upper or lower band, it statistically represents a 95% deviation from the mean, signaling an extreme overextension.

fields.name: ⚡ The Volatility Squeeze
fields.value: When the bands contract tightly together, institutional volatility has dried up. This 'squeeze' is the ultimate precursor to an explosive, directional breakout. You do not trade the squeeze; you wait for the violent expansion that follows.

modules.title: ☀️ Strategy Execution 1: The Band Squeeze Breakout
modules.description: Today we build a rigid execution protocol around the volatility squeeze.

fields.name: 🤖 Mechanical Ruleset
fields.value:
1. Identify a 4H Bollinger Band Squeeze.
2. Wait for a 1H candle to close aggressively outside the bands.
3. Enter on the retest of the broken SMA baseline.

fields.name: 💾 Data Logging
fields.value: Track the width of the bands prior to the breakout and log this numerical state output directly into your `signals.db` database. This allows you to quantitatively query how tight a squeeze needs to be to guarantee a profitable expansion.

modules.title: 🌙 Indicator Lab 2: Institutional Flow (VWAP)
modules.description: Retail uses standard moving averages. Institutions use the Volume Weighted Average Price.

fields.name: ⚖️ Price Meets Volume
fields.value: VWAP acts as the true baseline of the market because it weighs price action by trading volume. If a massive institution is accumulating a position, they execute strictly at or below the VWAP to avoid paying a premium.

fields.name: 🛑 The Institutional Boundary
fields.value: In an uptrending market, VWAP acts as an impenetrable dynamic support. If price breaks and sustains below the VWAP on high volume, the institutional bias has officially flipped.

modules.title: ☀️ Strategy Execution 2: VWAP Pullback Confluence
modules.description: Today we combine volume weighting with structural analysis for high-probability entries.

fields.name: 🤖 Mechanical Ruleset
fields.value:
1. Verify the Daily trend is bullish.
2. Wait for a short-term pullback on the 1H chart.
3. Execute a Long position strictly when price touches the VWAP line simultaneously with a fixed horizontal support zone.

fields.name: 📊 Testing Protocol
fields.value: Backtest this setup across 20 historical occurrences on GBP/USD. Ensure your data logs reflect whether volume spiked during the VWAP rejection.

modules.title: 🌙 Indicator Lab 3: Smart Money Concepts (SMC) & Liquidity
modules.description: Indicators lag. Liquidity leads. Tonight we map out where retail stop-losses are clustered.

fields.name: 🧲 Liquidity Pools
fields.value: Areas above Double Tops and below Double Bottoms are massive liquidity pools (clusters of retail stop-losses). Smart Money explicitly drives price into these zones to trigger those stops, absorbing the liquidity required to reverse the market.

fields.name: 🧹 The Sweep
fields.value: A 'Sweep' occurs when price violently spikes through a major support/resistance line, triggers stops, and immediately snaps back in the opposite direction. This is a manufactured false breakout.

modules.title: ☀️ Strategy Execution 3: The Liquidity Sweep Reversal
modules.description: Today we build a mechanical strategy designed to trade alongside the market makers, not against them.

fields.name: 🤖 Mechanical Ruleset
fields.value:
1. Identify an obvious Double Top (Retail Resistance).
2. Wait for price to break above the Double Top and sweep liquidity.
3. Enter a Short position immediately upon a 15-minute candle closing back *below* the original resistance line.

fields.name: 🛡️ Risk Parameters
fields.value: Place the Stop-Loss exactly 2 pips above the highest wick of the fakeout sweep. Target the opposing liquidity pool (the nearest Swing Low) for Take-Profit.

modules.title: 🌙 Indicator Lab 4: Algorithmic Multi-Indicator Confluence
modules.description: Tonight we define how to layer disparate data streams into a single, cohesive trade signal without muddying the chart.

fields.name: ⚙️ Non-Correlated Stacking
fields.value: Never stack indicators that measure the same thing (e.g., RSI and MACD are both momentum oscillators; using both is redundant). Instead, stack a trend filter (EMA), a volatility gauge (Bollinger), and a volume metric (VWAP).

fields.name: 📐 The Mathematical Formula
fields.value: Consider the VWAP calculation you are visually trading against: $$VWAP = \frac{\sum (Price \times Volume)}{\sum Volume}$$ By tracking the delta between current price and VWAP, you can programmatically define 'overextended' setups.

modules.title: ☀️ Strategy Execution 4: The Apex Confluence Model
modules.description: The final execution module. We combine momentum, volume, and structure into a fully mechanical pipeline.

fields.name: 🤖 The Ultimate Setup Checklist
fields.value:
1. Price is pulling back into a 4H Fair Value Gap (Structure).
2. Price rejects off the daily VWAP (Volume).
3. RSI prints clear bullish divergence on the 1H timeframe (Momentum).

fields.name: 💾 System Integration
fields.value: As you forward-test this model, ensure every generated signal is captured in `signals.db`. Tracking the Boolean states (True/False) of these three variables at the time of entry will allow you to quickly pivot this manual system into an automated script.
